title: HypothesisResponse
description: Schema for LLM-generated hypothesis review responses produced by the cellauto-lab Manual AI Hypothesis Loop. All responses must conform to this schema before being recorded. See docs/ai-hypothesis-loop.md for the workflow and prompts/hypothesis-review.md for the prompt template.

review_id.description: Unique identifier for this review session. Recommended format: review_YYYYMMDD_NNN.

source_summary_ids.description: List of experiment_id values from the summaries that were submitted for review. Must be non-empty.

candidate_rules.description: Wolfram rule numbers (0–255) that the LLM flagged as anomalous or interesting based on the submitted summaries. May be empty if no rules were flagged.

hypotheses.description: List of falsifiable hypotheses proposed by the LLM. May be empty if no hypothesis meeting quality standards could be proposed.

review_notes.description: Optional free-text notes from the LLM explaining why hypotheses could not be proposed, or recording any session-level observations. Only present when hypotheses is empty or when the LLM has session-level caveats.

Hypothesis.title: Hypothesis
Hypothesis.description: A single falsifiable hypothesis proposed by the LLM based on one or more experiment summaries.

hypothesis_id.description: Short unique identifier for this hypothesis within the review session. Recommended format: h001, h002, etc.

rule_ids.description: Wolfram rule numbers this hypothesis applies to. Must name at least one specific rule.

observation_basis.description: The specific metric values or observations from the submitted summaries that this hypothesis is based on. Must reference named metrics and their values. Must not contain interpretation — only what was measured.

hypothesis.description: The falsifiable claim, stated precisely. Must name specific rules, specific metrics, and a predicted direction of change. Must not use words like 'proves' or 'confirms'.

expected_result.description: The specific metric value or comparison that the follow-up simulation should produce if the hypothesis is correct. Must be concrete enough to evaluate as confirmed or refuted.

test_plan.description: The specific parameter change or simulate() call needed to test this hypothesis. Must reference concrete parameter values (n_cells, n_steps, initial_condition, boundary_condition, random_seed as applicable).

metrics_to_compare.description: Named metrics from the v1 metric set that should be compared between baseline and follow-up to evaluate the hypothesis. Must use exact metric names from compute_metrics() output.

confidence.description: The LLM's stated confidence in this hypothesis. 'high' requires extreme metric values with no alternative explanation. 'medium' requires plausibility with untested factors. 'low' is appropriate for speculative or single-run claims.

risk_of_overclaiming.description: An honest assessment of where this hypothesis might be misleading or wrong. Common risks: finite-size effects, boundary artifacts, transient behavior, single-initial-condition limitation.

status.description: Review status. LLM responses must always return 'proposed'. Human reviewers update to 'accepted_for_testing', 'rejected', or 'inconclusive' after applying the hypothesis quality checklist.

rejection_reason.description: Required when status is 'rejected'. Records why the hypothesis was rejected during human review. Set by the human reviewer, not the LLM.
